description: A designed statistics/infographic card with a clean white background and navy serif typography, headlined 'THE BIDEN JOB MARKET' (underlined). A subhead in italic reads 'Change in Employment Level August 2023-2024.' The centerpiece is an embedded FRED (Federal Reserve Economic Data) bar chart titled with a legend distinguishing 'Employment Level - Native Born' (light blue bars) from 'Employment Level - Foreign Born' (gray bars), plotted monthly from Aug 2023 to Aug 2024; the native-born bars are mostly negative (dipping to about -2,000 thousand around Dec 2023) while foreign-born bars are mostly positive, with footnotes 'Source: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics via FRED' and 'fred.stlouisfed.org.' Below the chart, two large contrasting figures drive the message home: 'NATIVE-BORN WORKERS: -1,319,000' and 'FOREIGN-BORN WORKERS: +1,240,000,' with the numbers in bold navy. Footer credits: 'Sources: BLS, Federal Reserve Bank, Fox News.' Visual style: authoritative, data-driven, formal serif design. Propaganda technique: selecting a specific 12-month window and juxtaposing native-born job losses against foreign-born gains to argue the Biden administration prioritized foreign workers over Americans, contrasted (per the tweet) with Trump putting 'AMERICANS FIRST.' Target audience: American workers and voters.
notable_text: THE BIDEN JOB MARKET / Change in Employment Level August 2023-2024 / NATIVE-BORN WORKERS: -1,319,000 / FOREIGN-BORN WORKERS: +1,240,000
